status_explanation: Automated/tier-plumbing half (T1) DONE + manager-gate-verified (mock suite 132 passed/1 deselected, exit 0; real.json still all-empty fail-closed; double-gate + allowlist + preflight + executable_path landed in commit 3693388). Real-site half BLOCKED at T2 logged-out preflight: headed Chromium 149 launched cleanly via /usr/bin/chromium (NO display issue, NO playwright/chromium protocol mismatch — both prior risks RESOLVED), profile UNLOCKED, but chatgpt.com showed a login/auth wall. Worker correctly refused to automate login and stopped. 0 real messages spent; 30-message budget fully intact. T2b/T3/T4 all depend on a signed-in session, so all blocked. EXACT operator action required: sign into chatgpt.com in the Chromium profile used for the run (~/.config/chromium, Default), then resume M-006 from T2.

manager_lifetimes.note: Canonical sibling primary, watched by team-lead watcher PID 3311983 (bounded auto-recovery). Recorded pid; created resume audit ledger tmp/real-audit-20260612T111042/messages.log (0 data lines; prior ledger also 0; budget 30/30 intact). Operator-confirmed signed-in profile = Chromium 'Profile 1' (the profile named 'agent') — to be INDEPENDENTLY verified by T2-pre (zero-message render probe) before any of the 30-message budget is authorized. Prior BLOCKER root cause: T2 worker targeted --profile-directory=Default (logged out); fix = target 'Profile 1'.

objective: Plumb an opt-in, bounded, audited real-site test tier (D-002); empirically discover real chatgpt.com selectors/affordances and fill real.json; run UC1-3 real-site acceptance; independently verify (best-of-N) and update VERIFICATION.md real-site scope with a final VERDICT.

real_message_budget.note: Budget spans ALL manager lifetimes AND both ledgers (sum). Recompute on resume = lines(prior)+lines(resume). Every real message logged BEFORE send. Exhausted -> finish verifiable, handoff PARTIAL, NEVER exceed.

constraints.real_site: Permitted ONLY in T2/T3 within budget. Headed only, human-paced, ONE real worker at a time (serialize). Login NEVER automated; logged-out/profile-locked -> named actionable error, STOP leg. Never read/store/log credentials/cookies/tokens/profile contents. No account identifiers in any artifact/report/commit/code.
constraints.default_tier: Stays loopback-mock-only; autouse socket guard must never be weakened; clean `uv run pytest` collects ZERO real_site tests.
constraints.write_scope: Only inside /home/abhmul/dev/ask-chatgpt (+ tmp/). Archive READ-ONLY. Never write .claude/ or .agents/. Never touch shared agent venv. uv sync --all-groups ALWAYS. Serialize pytest. NEVER git push.
constraints.real_legs_serialize: Real-site legs never overlap. pi launches serialize on this harness anyway (budget N-panel as ~sum).

blockers.task: T2 + T3 (automated real-site legs) and their dependents T2b/T4
blockers.condition: chatgpt.com serves a Cloudflare 'Just a moment...' bot-detection interstitial to the Playwright-automated headed Chromium (system binary, operator's real profile). Confirmed by 3 zero-message render probes (Profile 1 + Default): the interstitial persists the full 45s and NEVER clears to the app or a login page. The automated browser cannot reach chatgpt.com's app OR auth layer at all. Passing it would require bot-detection evasion (stealth flags / webdriver patching), which is outward-facing, not authorized under D-002, plausibly contrary to OpenAI ToS for the operator account, and disallowed by the charter — the agent did NOT attempt it.
blockers.agent_cannot_resolve: Correct: not an auth problem (operator's own browser is signed in and passes Cloudflare; automation just can't reach auth). 'Sign in again' would NOT help. Evasion is out of scope.
decision_options: DEFAULT/recommended: revert real-site proof to operator-run runbooks (charter anchor: 'Real-chatgpt.com proof is operator-run from runbooks, never automated'); mock tier (132 passing) stays authoritative; T1 stands as the automated deliverable; real.json stays empty/fail-closed.

OPTIONAL: operator launches their OWN Chromium with --remote-debugging-port=9222 (passes Cloudflare as a real human browser, already signed in) and a worker connect_over_cdp()s to it — needs operator consent + may re-challenge + more complex.
NOT recommended w/o explicit operator sign-off: stealth/anti-detection (ToS + charter risk).
blockers.evidence: orchestration/reports/M-006/RESUME-FINDING-cloudflare.md + reports/M-006/T2-pre.md, T2-pre-v2.md, T2-pre-v3.md (v3 timeline: title 'Just a moment...' sec2->44, composer=0 login_ctl=0 throughout).
blockers.budget_impact: none — 0 of 30 real messages spent; full budget intact.
blockers.exact_operator_action: Decide among the options above and tell the team lead. If DEFAULT: no further automated real-site work; close M-006 with T1 delivered + real proof delegated to operator/runbooks; optionally run T1 independent best-of-N verification. If CDP: operator starts signed-in Chromium with --remote-debugging-port=9222 and notifies the lead to re-dispatch a CDP-attach discovery worker.

blockers.condition: REFUTED 2026-06-12 by manager-2: prior 'Default profile logged out / sign in to resume' diagnosis was wrong. Ground-truth re-probe shows the real blocker is the Cloudflare interstitial (B2), which sits BEFORE the auth layer — login state is unobservable via automation, so it was never the actual blocker. The original B1 text/operator-action below is retained for history only; do NOT act on it.
blockers.original_condition: The Chromium Default profile (~/.config/chromium) is logged out of chatgpt.com (login/auth wall). Login is NEVER automated (D-002), so the agent cannot resolve this.
blockers.exact_operator_action: Sign into chatgpt.com in the Chromium profile at ~/.config/chromium (Default profile), keeping that profile signed in. Then close Chromium (so the profile is UNLOCKED for the headed worker) and resume MISSION-006 — the resume manager re-dispatches T2 from preflight. If your signed-in ChatGPT session lives in a DIFFERENT Chromium profile-directory, say which one so T2 can target it via --profile-directory (the agent will not inspect your profiles).
blockers.not_blocked_on: display reachability (DISPLAY=:0 worked), profile lock (was unlocked), or playwright/chromium version (149 drove cleanly) — these are RESOLVED. Only sign-in remains.
blockers.budget_impact: none — 0 of 30 real messages spent; full budget available on resume.

resume_instructions: Re-verify recorded state vs ground truth INCLUDING messages spent (count lines in the audit ledger tmp/real-audit-20260612T100518/messages.log; budget spans all manager lifetimes). For each task with status DONE, confirm the named report exists with its STATUS line before skipping it. Continue from the first non-DONE task respecting deps. Real legs serialize and require the profile UNLOCKED (no Chromium running) + a reachable display + logged-in profile; if any precondition fails at T2/T3, complete what is verifiable and handoff PARTIAL with the EXACT operator action. NEVER exceed 30 real messages. NEVER git push. Headless claude -p: foreground watches only (--wait-seconds 480, loop), never background; write handoff before ending turn.
next_action: MISSION PAUSED — PARTIAL/BLOCKED on the Cloudflare bot interstitial (blocker B2); requires an operator/team-lead DECISION, not a sign-in. This headless manager closed out: 3 zero-message probes ran (budget still 0/30), Cloudflare confirmed, finding + handoff written, slice committed. RESUME PATHS by decision: (A DEFAULT) revert real proof to operator-run runbooks — no further automated real-site work; T1 is the delivered automated artifact; the one remaining UNBLOCKED automated task is the T1 independent best-of-N verification panel (T4-style, mock-only, non-real) -> then close M-006 DONE-for-automated-scope with real proof delegated to the operator. (B) CDP-attach: operator launches their signed-in Chromium with --remote-debugging-port=9222; a fresh manager authors a CDP-attach variant of T2/T3 (connect_over_cdp instead of launch_persistent_context) honoring the same SAFETY BLOCK + 30-msg budget, then T2b/T4 as before. (C) stealth: NOT without explicit operator sign-off. Do NOT re-run the logged-out resume path (B1 is REFUTED). The proven launch mechanics (executable_path=/usr/bin/chromium, user_data_dir=~/.config/chromium, headed, DISPLAY=:0) are fine; the wall is Cloudflare, which sits before auth.
